title: C++ Function Pointer Generator
scenario: Generate a C++ function pointer that takes a string as input and returns an integer. The function pointer should be declared as a pointer to a function that takes a string and returns an integer. The function pointer should be initialized with a lambda function that simply returns 0. The function pointer should be used in a context where it is passed as an argument to another function.
constraints: Must be declared as a pointer to a function that takes a string and returns an integer
Must be initialized with a lambda function that returns 0

evaluationCriteria.description: Check if the function pointer adheres to the declaration and initialization constraints.
Verify that the function pointer has correct C++ syntax.
Check if the function pointer is used correctly in the context where it is passed as an argument.
